Coagulation

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows normocytic anemia (low hemoglobin), elevated RDW, and significant renal dysfunction (high creatinine). There is marked hyperbilirubinemia and mildly elevated ALT, suggesting possible liver dysfunction or hemolysis. Severe metabolic acidosis (very low arterial pH) and mild hypoxemia are present. Glucose is low. These findings indicate acute or chronic multi-organ dysfunction, requiring urgent clinical evaluation and management.
